questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: "She ___ to school every day."
questions.explanation: "every day" signals a repeated habit, so we use the present simple. With the third-person singular "she", we add -es to a verb ending in -o: she goes. "is going" (present continuous) is for actions happening now; "go" forgets the -es needed after she/he/it; "going" alone is not a finite verb.

"Look!" draws attention to something happening right now, so we use the present continuous (is + -ing): the baby is crying. "cries" and "cry" are present simple (habits, not this moment); "cried" is past simple.

questions.prompt: Which word signals the present simple?
questions.explanation: Adverbs of frequency such as "usually" describe how often something happens, so they signal a habit and the present simple. By contrast, "now", "at the moment", and "Look!" all point to an action in progress and go with the present continuous.

"on Sundays" signals a regular habit, so we use the present simple. The third-person singular "he" adds -s to the verb: he plays. "play" is missing the -s; "is play" mixes two verb forms; "playing" alone cannot be the main verb without am/is/are.

"know" is a stative verb (a mental state, not an action), so it is not used in the continuous: "I am knowing" is wrong. With "I" we use the base form with no -s, so "I knows" is also wrong, and "I am know" mixes two verbs. The correct sentence is the simple present: I know the answer.
"at the moment" signals now, so we use the present continuous with the plural subject they: are watching.
